sections.paragraph:
ἀνίστημι [ἀνά, ἵστημι]

—1. tr. ‘to cause to rise up’ raise up

—a. of someone in a recumbent position and caused to stand Ac 9:41 (cp. the usage in 2 below).

—b. of a deceased pers. J 6:39f; Ac 2:24, 32; 13:34; cp. the denial of such possibility in Sophocles, Electra 138f.

—c. of an entity that is ‘raised up’ to fill a void Mt 22:24; Ac 3:22, 26.

—2. intr. in mid. and 2 aor. act. rise (up), get up

—a. of someone in a recumbent position Mk 1:35; Lk 11:7.

—b. of the dead Mt 17:23 v.l.; Mk 8:31; w. ἐκ νεϰρῶν Mt 17:9 v.l.; 1 Th 4:16.

—c. of someone taking a standing position Mt 26:62.

—d. of someone rising in prelude to leaving a place Mt 9:9; here the context indicates a specific position, but the more general sense ‘get up’ w. a verb of motion, as in our ‘get up and go’, appears in Mk 10:1; Lk 1:39; Ac 8:26.

—e. of someone making an appearance, rise, appear Ac 5:36; Ro 15:12.
